text: {"claims":[{"statement":"UNILAG was founded in 1962","claimType":"empirical","quote":"UNILAG was founded in 1962, two years after the independence of Nigeria from Britain.","entailment":"The quoted sentence explicitly states the founding year of UNILAG as 1962.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"UNILAG was one of the first five universities created in that UNILAG belonged to the initial five universities established in Nigeria.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eni Njoku was appointed as the first black when he was replaced by Saburi Biobaku.","entailment":"The sentence specifies both the appointment of Eni Njoku as the first black vice-chancellor and the year 1962.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Saburi Biobaku was stabbed by Kayode Adams","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Saburi was stabbed by Kayode Adams, a student radical who believed Biobaku's appointment was unfair and ethnically motivated.","entailment":"The quoted clause reports the stabbing of Saburi Biobaku by Kayode Adams.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"On 29 May 2012 Goodluck Jonathan proposed to rename the University of Lagos to Moshood Abiola University","claimType":"empirical","quote":"On 29 May 2012, the then President of Nigeria, Goodluck Jonathan, proposed to rename the University of Lagos to Moshood Abiola University in honour of Moshood Abiola, who died in jail as a political prisoner in 1998.","entailment":"The quote records the date and the proposal by Goodluck Jonathan to change the university's name.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The proposed name change became a subject of protests from alumni protested the name change proposal.","population":"students and alumni","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"protests","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The Nigerian federal government gave in to the protests incited by the proposed name change","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The proposal was consequently jettisoned as the Nigerian federal government gave in to the protests incited by the proposed name change.","entailment":"The quote indicates that the government withdrew the proposal due to the protests.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"proposal jettisoned","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"A visitation panel detected cases of financial abuses from top officials between 2016 and 2020","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A visitation panel, created to look into the affairs of the university between 2016 and 2020 detected cases of financial abuses from top officials and ordered the university to close accounts with commercial banks.","entailment":"The sentence reports that the panel identified financial abuses during the 2016‑2020 period.","population":"top
